title: Generative AI
definition: Generative AI refers to machine-learning systems that produce or transform media such as text, image, audio, video, code or structured data from learned patterns.
abstract: Generative AI connects machine learning, creative workflows, latent spaces, risk management, provenance, authorship and cultural production.
description: A canonical concept record for generative AI in creative and knowledge systems.
exclusions: Rule-based generation with no learned model
Claims of autonomous creativity that erase human, dataset and infrastructure roles
claims: Generative AI should be evaluated through provenance, constraints, use context and human judgment.
Creative use of generative AI is stronger when it documents prompts, sources, edits, selection and rights.
bodyHtml:
Definition

Generative AI describes learned systems that produce or transform media. The output may be text, image, sound, video, code or structured data.

Scope

The concept includes foundation models, latent spaces, diffusion, transformers, prompts, embeddings, datasets, evaluation, provenance and human editing.

Applications

Electronic Artefacts can use generative AI as a research topic and production method when outputs remain connected to sources, constraints and interpretation.

Limitations

Generative AI can obscure training data, authorship, rights and confidence. Responsible practice documents process and avoids claiming certainty where the system provides probability.

References

See NIST AI RMF, provenance, generative systems and augmented intelligence.
